title: Fix+Test: revoked API key still returned by GET /keys/{id}
description:
fetchKey() in lambdas/auth/main.go does not filter WHERE revoked_at IS NULL. Only the list endpoint (fetchKeys) filters revoked keys. A deleted/revoked key is still returned by GET /keys/{keyId} with no indication it is revoked.

What to fix:
- Add revoked_at IS NULL condition to the single-key fetchKey query, OR
- Return the key with revoked_at field populated so callers can detect revocation state
- Decide and document which behavior is correct (404 vs 200 with revoked_at)

What to test:
- Create key → DELETE key → GET key by ID → assert 404 (if hiding revoked) or assert revoked_at is set (if showing state)
- Verify revoked key cannot authenticate (401)

title: Fix+Test: PATCH /messages/{id} not implemented (zero coverage)
description:
PATCH /inboxes/{inboxId}/threads/{threadId}/messages/{messageId} is documented in api/openapi.yaml (update unread, starred, labels on a message) but has no case in lambdas/messages/main.go switch statement.

What to implement:
- Add PATCH handler for individual message update
- Support updating is_read, is_starred fields
- Support adding/removing label associations

What to test:
- Send message → PATCH is_read=false → GET message → assert is_read=false
- PATCH is_starred=true → GET → assert is_starred=true
- Verify 404 on nonexistent message ID

title: Fix+Test: forward endpoint not implemented (zero coverage)
description:
POST /inboxes/{inboxId}/threads/{threadId}/messages/{messageId}/forward exists in api/openapi.yaml but has no case in lambdas/messages/main.go. Callers get a silent 404.

What to implement:
- Add forward handler in the messages lambda
- Create new message in a new thread with original content in body
- Include original attachments
- Publish message.sent domain event

What to test:
- Send message with attachment → forward to different address → verify new message in new thread
- Verify original content preserved in body
- Verify attachment included in forwarded message
- Verify message.sent event fires

title: Fix+Test: reply-all endpoint not implemented (zero coverage)
description:
POST /inboxes/{inboxId}/threads/{threadId}/messages/{messageId}/reply-all exists in api/openapi.yaml but has no case in lambdas/messages/main.go. Callers get a silent 404 with no diagnostic.

What to implement:
- Add reply-all handler in the messages lambda
- Populate RFC 5322 In-Reply-To and References headers from the original message
- Create new message in same thread
- Publish message.sent domain event

What to test:
- Create inbox → send message → reply-all → verify new message in same thread
- Verify In-Reply-To and References headers match original message_id
- Verify message.sent event fires via webhook delivery record

title: Fix: scope enforcement missing on 80% of API surface
description:
Only the inboxes lambda calls HasScope(). Every other lambda — drafts, messages, threads, labels, search, webhooks, org, pods, keys, domains — reads JWT claims but never enforces scopes. A key with inbox:read only can create webhooks, approve drafts, and mutate threads.

What to fix:
- Add HasScope checks to all write endpoints in every lambda that currently lacks them
- Verify each resource against the scope list in pkg/auth/scopes.go (org:admin, pod:admin, inbox:read/write, webhook:read/write, draft:read/write, search:read)

What to test:
- For each resource, create a scoped key with insufficient permissions and verify 403 on write operations
- Confirm reads succeed with read-only scope
close_reason: Implemented and deployed. Scope guards added to all 8 lambda handlers. Thread filter params (?unread, ?starred, ?label) wired. Integration tests pass (2 runs).

title: Fix wrong SES EventBridge detail-type strings (production events silently dropped)
description:
## What's broken

The EventBridge rule filter in terraform/eventbridge.tf and the Lambda switch/case in lambdas/ses_events/main.go both use incorrect event type strings that do not match the actual AWS SES EventBridge events.

**Wrong strings in use:**
- "SES Bounce" → actual: "Email Bounced"
- "SES Complaint" → actual: "Email Complaint Received"
- "SES Message Delivery" → actual: "Email Delivered"

**Impact:** In production, EventBridge drops all SES events because the filter never matches. The ses_events Lambda never runs. Message statuses are permanently stuck at 'sending' — they never transition to 'sent', 'failed', or 'bounced'.

## What needs to be done

1. **terraform/eventbridge.tf**: Update detail-type filter array to use correct strings
2. **lambdas/ses_events/main.go**: Update switch/case and struct comment
3. **tests/integration/ses_events_test.go**: Update synthetic event payloads
4. **lambdas/ses_events/main_test.go**: Update unit test event type strings

## Acceptance criteria
- EventBridge rule matches actual AWS SES event types
- Lambda correctly routes Email Bounced, Email Complaint Received, Email Delivered
- Integration tests inject events with correct detail-type strings
- terraform plan shows updated filter, terraform apply deploys it

title: Authorizer Lambda: API key validation returning IAM policy
description: Lambda authorizer for API GW REST API. Input: TOKEN type event with Authorization: Bearer <key>. Validate key against Aurora DB. Return IAM Allow/Deny policy with context: org_id, pod_id, scopes. Must work outside VPC (uses public RDS Proxy endpoint). Critical path - blocks all API testing.

title: Lambda build infra: Makefile targets, provided.al2023 runtime, Terraform update
description: 1) Add lambdas/ Go module to go.work. 2) Makefile build-lambdas: compile each Lambda to bootstrap binary + zip, upload to S3 artifacts bucket. 3) Update all 19 Lambda Terraform resources: runtime=provided.al2023, handler=bootstrap, source from S3 zip. 4) Add aws-lambda-go dependency.

title: SQS publisher package: pkg/sqs with multi-queue event fanout
description: Create pkg/sqs package that implements EventPublisher interface. Must publish events to multiple queues (webhook_delivery, ws_dispatch, embedder) based on event type. Env vars: WEBHOOK_DELIVERY_QUEUE_URL, WS_DISPATCH_QUEUE_URL, EMBEDDER_QUEUE_URL. Use AWS SDK v2.

title: Publisher interface: abstract EventPublisher from kafka.Producer
description: Services hardcode *kafka.Producer for event publishing. Lambda functions need SQS instead. Create EventPublisher interface in pkg/events or pkg/publisher, implement with kafka.Producer (existing) and sqs.Publisher (new). Update all service constructors to accept interface.

title: Fix+Test: invalid pagination cursor should return 400 across list endpoints
description: Store layers return explicit invalid-cursor errors, but list handlers commonly translate these to generic 500 responses. Integration tests do not cover malformed cursor inputs, so API error semantics are currently unguarded.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Invalid cursor returns 400 (not 500) for inbox/thread/message/draft list endpoints. 2) Error responses are consistent and documented. 3) Integration tests add malformed cursor cases for each affected endpoint.

title: Fix+Test: enforce parent-path integrity for nested inbox/thread/message/draft routes
description: Several nested handlers parse parent path params (inboxId/threadId) but operate only on child IDs, which risks cross-resource access when path parent IDs do not match the child’s actual ownership. Integration tests currently only exercise happy-path matching IDs.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Nested endpoints verify parent-child relationships (inboxId/threadId vs resource owner). 2) Mismatched parent IDs return 404 (or 400, if intentionally chosen and documented) consistently. 3) Integration tests cover negative mismatched-ID cases for threads, messages, and drafts.

title: Fix+Test: PATCH /org does not persist updates and integration test misses read-after-write
description: PATCH /v1/org currently mutates only the in-memory org object returned from fetch without persisting to the database, while integration coverage checks only status code and not durable state. This can return success without actual update persistence.
acceptance_criteria: 1) PATCH /v1/org persists updated fields to the organizations table. 2) GET /v1/org after PATCH returns the updated values. 3) Integration test adds read-after-write assertion to prevent regression.

title: Test+Doc: inbox single GET is org-scoped, not pod-scoped (unlike list)
description:
GET /inboxes/{id} filters only by org_id. GET /inboxes (list) enforces pod isolation. This means a pod-2 key can directly GET a pod-1 inbox by ID — intentional behavior but undocumented and untested.

The RLS isolation test covers list but not single-GET. Without an explicit test, a future fix to add pod filtering to single-GET could be viewed as a bug or regression with no guard.

What to test:
- Create pod-1 inbox and pod-2 key
- GET /inboxes/{pod1-inbox-id} with pod-2 key
- Assert the expected result (200 returns inbox OR 403/404 if behavior should change)
- Document the decision in the test comment

title: Fix+Test: GET /inboxes?pod_id= filter param silently ignored
description:
api/openapi.yaml documents ?pod_id= as a filter on GET /inboxes. lambdas/inboxes/main.go reads claims.PodID for RLS but never reads event.QueryStringParameters[pod_id]. An org-admin key passing ?pod_id= gets all inboxes instead of the filtered set.

What to fix:
- Read ?pod_id= from query params in the inboxes lambda
- Apply the filter to the DB query when an org-admin key provides it

What to test:
- Create org-admin key + 2 inboxes in different pods
- GET /inboxes?pod_id=pod1 → assert only pod1 inboxes returned
- GET /inboxes?pod_id=pod2 → assert only pod2 inboxes returned

title: Test: draft state-machine — cannot update/approve already-approved draft
description:
Tests verify: cannot approve a rejected draft. Missing cases:
- Cannot approve an already-approved draft (second approve attempt)
- Cannot PATCH (update) a draft that has already been approved
- Cannot PATCH a draft that has been rejected

The service enforces existing.ReviewStatus != DraftReviewStatusPending before Update, Approve, and Reject but these paths have no integration test coverage.

What to test:
- Create draft → approve → approve again → assert 400/409
- Create draft → approve → PATCH fields → assert 400/409
- Create draft → reject → PATCH fields → assert 400/409

title: Fix+Test: webhook auth_header custom injection not implemented or tested
description:
api/openapi.yaml documents auth_header.name and auth_header.value fields on POST /webhooks for receiver-side authentication. The create webhook handler accepts the field in the request but no Webhook model field stores it and no dispatch code injects a custom header into deliveries. The value should also be write-only (not returned in GET responses).

What to fix (if not implemented):
- Store auth_header.name and auth_header.value (encrypted) on the webhook record
- Inject the custom header into every outgoing webhook delivery

What to test:
- Create webhook with auth_header → GET webhook → assert value is absent (write-only), name is present
- Trigger delivery → verify custom header was sent in the HTTP request (via local receiver or capturing receiver)

title: Test: GET /labels/{labelId} has zero coverage
description:
Tests cover label list, PATCH, and DELETE but never GET by ID. The endpoint exists in the OpenAPI spec.

What to test:
- Create label → GET /labels/{id} → assert all fields match (name, color, created_at)
- GET /labels/{nonexistent-id} → assert 404
- Verify label returned by ID matches the one returned in the list

title: Fix+Test: thread list filter params ?unread= ?starred= ?label= silently ignored
description:
api/openapi.yaml documents ?unread=, ?starred=, and ?label= as query parameters for GET /threads. lambdas/threads/main.go only reads ?status= and cursor/limit. The ThreadListQuery struct has LabelID, IsRead, IsStarred fields but the lambda never populates them from query params — all three filters silently do nothing.

What to fix:
- Wire ?unread= → IsRead, ?starred= → IsStarred, ?label= → LabelID in the threads lambda query-param parsing

What to test:
- Apply label to thread → GET /threads?label={labelId} → assert only labeled thread returned
- Mark thread unread → GET /threads?unread=true → assert appears; GET /threads?unread=false → assert absent
- Star thread → GET /threads?starred=true → assert appears
close_reason: Implemented and deployed. Scope guards added to all 8 lambda handlers. Thread filter params (?unread, ?starred, ?label) wired. Integration tests pass (2 runs).

title: Fix+Test: GET /messages/{id}/attachments/{id} not implemented (zero coverage)
description:
GET /inboxes/{inboxId}/threads/{threadId}/messages/{messageId}/attachments/{attachmentId} is in api/openapi.yaml but has no case in lambdas/messages/main.go. Tests verify attachments are stored (metadata fields, s3_key) but never that the binary can be retrieved.

What to implement:
- Add attachment download handler that fetches the binary from S3
- Return binary with correct Content-Type and Content-Disposition headers

What to test:
- Send message with attachment → GET attachment by ID → assert binary response, correct Content-Type, non-empty body
- Assert 404 on nonexistent attachment ID
- Assert 404 on attachment belonging to a different message

title: Fix+Test: GET /messages/{id}/raw not implemented (zero coverage)
description:
GET /inboxes/{inboxId}/threads/{threadId}/messages/{messageId}/raw is documented in api/openapi.yaml (returns RFC 5322 bytes, Content-Type: message/rfc822). No case exists in lambdas/messages/main.go.

What to implement:
- Add raw message handler that fetches the original .eml from S3
- Return raw bytes with Content-Type: message/rfc822

What to test:
- Inject inbound email → GET /messages/{id}/raw → assert Content-Type: message/rfc822
- Assert RFC 5322 headers (From, To, Subject, Message-ID) are present in response body
- Assert 404 on nonexistent message ID

title: Fat event payloads: embed full message in MessageReceived/Sent/Bounced events
description:
Replace sparse event data structs (IDs + a few fields) with a MessagePayload that carries the complete message snapshot. Consumers receive everything in one shot — no follow-up API call needed.

Fields included: id, message_id, inbox_id, thread_id, direction, status, subject, from, to, cc, bcc, reply_to, body_text, body_html, preview, headers, attachments (metadata only, no binary), sent_at, received_at, created_at, updated_at.

Touches: pkg/events/email_events.go, lambdas/email_inbound/main.go, lambdas/email_outbound/main.go, pkg/events/events_test.go

title: Differentiate SES bounce vs complaint: map to distinct internal statuses
description:
## Context

Currently ses_events Lambda maps both 'Email Bounced' and 'Email Complaint Received' to status='failed'. But the API MessageStatus enum explicitly defines 'bounced' as separate from 'failed', indicating these should be differentiated.

## Correct mapping

| SES EventBridge event     | Internal status | Rationale                                      |
|---------------------------|-----------------|------------------------------------------------|
| Email Bounced             | bounced         | Hard/soft bounce — email undeliverable          |
| Email Complaint Received  | failed          | Recipient marked as spam — policy/reputation   |
| Email Delivered           | sent            | Confirmed delivery (unchanged)                 |

## What needs to be done

1. Update lambdas/ses_events/main.go: Email Bounced → models.MessageStatusBounced
2. Update lambdas/ses_events/main.go: Email Complaint Received → models.MessageStatusFailed
3. Update unit and integration tests to assert correct status per event type
4. Ensure OpenAPI MessageStatus enum documents the distinction

## Dependencies
Blocked by nGX-dhh (DB constraint must include 'bounced' first)

## Acceptance criteria
- Bounce events set status='bounced', complaint events set status='failed'
- Tests assert distinct statuses

title: DB migration: add 'bounced' to messages.status CHECK constraint
description:
## What's broken

The messages table CHECK constraint in migrations/000005_create_messages.up.sql allows:
  status IN ('received', 'sending', 'sent', 'failed', 'draft')

But pkg/models/types.go defines MessageStatusBounced = "bounced" and openapi.yaml includes 'bounced' in the MessageStatus enum. Writing status='bounced' to the DB will cause a constraint violation at runtime.

Additionally, 'draft' is in the DB constraint but absent from the OpenAPI MessageStatus enum — a minor inconsistency.

## What needs to be done

1. Create new migration (e.g. 000011_messages_status_bounced.up.sql) that alters the CHECK constraint to include 'bounced'
2. Down migration must restore the old constraint
3. Verify existing rows are unaffected (no existing 'bounced' rows to migrate)
4. Reconcile 'draft' status: either add it to OpenAPI enum or remove it from DB constraint (coordinate with API team)

## Acceptance criteria
- Migration applies cleanly on top of existing schema
- messages.status accepts 'bounced' without error
- Go model and OpenAPI enum are in sync with DB constraint

title: Integration test: cursor pagination for messages and threads
description:
Cursor pagination is exercised incidentally but no test verifies multi-page traversal, cursor stability, or boundary behavior. An off-by-one in cursor encoding could silently drop messages.

What to test:
- Send 3+ messages to an inbox; list with limit=2 → verify nextCursor returned
- Use nextCursor to fetch page 2 → verify remaining messages appear, no duplicates
- Verify final page returns no nextCursor
- Test with invalid/expired cursor → should return 400
- Same for thread listing with limit parameter

title: Integration test: domain verify endpoint (pending→active status)
description:
POST /v1/domains/{id}/verify is fully implemented but never exercised. The S3 env var bug found during TestCustomDomainProvisioning shows how untested code paths hide real bugs. The verify flow polls SES for DNS propagation and transitions status pending→verifying→active.

What to test:
- Register a domain (mail.nyklabs.com) → status=pending
- Call POST /v1/domains/{id}/verify immediately (DNS not set) → status stays pending or transitions to verifying
- Verify response includes DNS records for re-display
- Cleanup via DELETE

Note: full active transition requires DNS to be set — that part is manual only.

title: Integration test: thread status transitions (open/closed/spam/trash)
description:
Only is_read and is_starred PATCH fields are tested. Thread status (open/closed/spam/trash) transitions are completely untested despite affecting list filtering behavior.

What to test:
- PATCH thread status to closed → verify GET returns status=closed
- PATCH thread status to spam → verify it is excluded from default list
- PATCH thread status to trash → verify excluded from default list
- PATCH back to open → verify re-appears in default list
- Verify thread counts update correctly
close_reason: TestThreadStatusTransitions added and passing — 6 subtests cover initial_status_is_open, close, spam, trash, reopen, and invalid_status_rejected. List filter ?status= verified at each transition.

title: Integration test: thread label attach and detach
description:
Label CRUD is tested but attaching/detaching labels from threads is not. The code path from PUT/DELETE /v1/threads/{id}/labels is completely untouched by integration tests.

What to test:
- Create a label
- Attach label to a thread via PUT /v1/threads/{id}/labels
- Verify GET /v1/threads/{id} shows the label
- Detach label via DELETE /v1/threads/{id}/labels/{label_id}
- Verify label is removed from thread

title: Integration test: row-level security org isolation
description:
Postgres RLS is configured but no test explicitly verifies that org A cannot read org B's data. If RLS policies are misconfigured or bypassed, one org could silently access another's messages, threads, and drafts.

What to test:
- Create two separate org API keys
- Org A creates inbox + sends message
- Verify org B key gets 404/empty results on org A's resource IDs
- Test threads, messages, drafts, and attachments

title: Integration test: API key scope and pod isolation enforcement
description:
API keys have pod_id and scopes fields but no test verifies enforcement. A key scoped to pod A should be blocked from accessing pod B's inboxes/messages. This is a security boundary — if broken it won't be caught until a customer reports a data leak.

What to test:
- Create key with pod_id restriction; verify it cannot read another pod's inboxes
- Create key with inbox:read scope; verify it cannot POST to create messages
- Create key with no scopes; verify appropriate rejection
- Verify org-level isolation: key from org A gets 403/404 on org B resources

title: Outbound email attachment support
description: Send API and draft API both accept inline base64 attachments. email_outbound Lambda builds multipart/mixed MIME. Requires migration 000019 (draft_id on attachments table).
close_reason: Implemented inline base64 attachment support for outbound email and drafts. All lambdas deployed. Migration 000019 created — needs manual apply via SSM tunnel.

title: Embed outbound messages at send time
description:
Outbound messages (sent via API) are never embedded because body_text_key is only set by the inbound email pipeline. The embedder silently skips them (empty body_text_key). MessageSentEvent is published after SES delivery succeeds, which fails for unverified recipients in SES sandbox.

Fix: in the message_service Send path, after creating the message record, store the body text to S3 and publish a MessageSentEvent directly (same pattern as inbound). The email_outbound lambda should not publish MessageSentEvent (or it becomes a duplicate - needs deduplication).

Simpler alternative: store body text in S3 from Send(), and publish EventMessageSent from the messages lambda immediately after DB insert. Remove MessageSentEvent from email_outbound (it's redundant for embedding - SES delivery status tracked separately via ses_events).

Files to change:
- services/inbox/service/message_service.go: store body to S3, publish EventMessageSent after create
- lambdas/messages/main.go: pass S3 client + publisher to MessageService
- lambdas/email_outbound/main.go: remove duplicate MessageSentEvent publication
- tests/integration/search_test.go: TestSemanticSearch should now pass (no SES sandbox dependency)
close_reason: Fixed: MessageSentEvent now published from messages lambda at send time with inline BodyText. Embedder uses inline body for outbound messages, skipping S3 fetch. All 3 search tests pass.

title: Wire Cloudflare Workers AI as embedding backend
description:
The embedder and search lambdas have no live embedding service configured. Wire up Cloudflare Workers AI (bge-base-en-v1.5, 768D) as the backend.

What needs to change:
1. pkg/embedder/client.go — add apiKey field, send Authorization: Bearer header
2. lambdas/embedder/main.go — read EMBEDDER_API_KEY env var; fix hardcoded dims=768 (was inconsistent with vector(256) column)
3. lambdas/search/main.go — same API key + dims fix
4. New DB migration — change vector(256) → vector(768), drop+recreate HNSW index
5. Terraform — add EMBEDDER_API_KEY (from Secrets Manager) and EMBEDDER_DIMS env vars to embedder and search lambda modules

Cloudflare endpoint: https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/accounts/{ACCOUNT_ID}/ai/v1
Model: @cf/baai/bge-base-en-v1.5 (768D, OpenAI-compatible format — zero Go client protocol changes needed)

Bug fixed as part of this: dims hardcoded to 768 in both lambdas but column was vector(256) — would have failed on first real embed call.
close_reason: Implemented: apiKey field in embedder client, EMBEDDER_API_KEY+EMBEDDER_DIMS env vars in both lambdas, migration 000018 resizing vector column 256→768, terraform variables and lambda env vars updated, .env.example updated for Cloudflare Workers AI

title: Clean up legacy Docker/Kafka/Redis artifacts from main branch
description:
After merging aws-serverless-stack into main, legacy files from the old Docker microservices architecture were carried over. Need to remove: services/ directory (10 old microservices), docker-compose.yml, configs/ (Kafka/Nginx configs), docs/ (old architecture docs), pkg/kafka, pkg/redis, pkg/middleware, and update go.work, Makefile, and .env.example to remove all old references.

**Why:** The old main branch (identical to pkr-docker-stack) had Docker Compose, Kafka, Redis, custom API gateway, and self-hosted SMTP. These are all replaced by AWS Lambda, SQS, SES, and API Gateway. Keeping the old files creates confusion and build noise.

**How to apply:** Delete entire services/ tree, configs/, obsolete docs. Remove services/* from go.work. Update Makefile to only include Lambda-relevant targets. Purge KAFKA/REDIS/SMTP vars from .env.example.

title: Fix webhook event delivery: no delivery records after message send
description: Bug found during integration testing. After sending a message via API, no webhook delivery records appear for a registered webhook (subscribed to message.sent/thread.created) within 30s. Possible causes: (1) message.sent event not published to WEBHOOK_DELIVERY_QUEUE, (2) event_dispatcher_webhook not matching event types, (3) delivery record not written. Check event_dispatcher_webhook Lambda logs. TestWebhookEventDelivery and TestWebhookDeliveryRecord both fail.
close_reason: Fixed and verified by integration tests passing

title: Add scheduled_at column to drafts table (migration + deploy)
description:
Missing DB migration. models.Draft has ScheduledAt field and scheduler_drafts Lambda queries scheduled_at, but the column does not exist in the DB. TestSchedulerDrafts fails: ERROR: column scheduled_at does not exist (SQLSTATE 42703).

Steps: (1) Add migration: ALTER TABLE drafts ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS scheduled_at TIMESTAMPTZ; (2) Run via SSM tunnel psql against prod. (3) Rebuild/redeploy drafts Lambda (CreateDraftRequest and UpdateDraftRequest now have ScheduledAt field added in this session).
close_reason: Fixed and verified by integration tests passing

title: Fix drafts approve: draft persists after approve
description: Bug found during integration testing. POST /v1/inboxes/:id/drafts/:draftId/approve returns 200 but draft still appears in list after approve. Check drafts Lambda logs after approve call. DraftService.Approve should create a message and remove the draft — if draft persists, either the deletion failed or there is a transaction issue. May be related to the json tag changes in this session.
close_reason: Fixed and verified by integration tests passing

title: Fix inbox PATCH: display_name not persisted (missing JSON tag)
description: Bug found during integration testing. PATCH /v1/inboxes/:id sends display_name in snake_case but the request struct has no json tags — DisplayName never gets decoded. Same root cause as the SendMessageRequest body_text fix done in this session. Need to add json tags to InboxPatchRequest (or equivalent) in services/inbox, rebuild and redeploy inboxes Lambda.
close_reason: Fixed and verified by integration tests passing

title: Fix inbox PATCH: display_name not persisted (missing JSON tag)
description:
Bug found during integration testing. PATCH /v1/inboxes/:id sends display_name in snake_case but InboxPatchRequest struct has no json tags, so DisplayName never gets decoded. The patch silently succeeds but display_name is not updated. Same root cause as the SendMessageRequest/CreateDraftRequest json tag bug fixed in this session.

**Why:** Go json.Unmarshal uses case-insensitive matching for CamelCase fields (display_name doesn't match DisplayName). Need to add json:"display_name" tag to InboxPatchRequest and any other request structs used by the inboxes Lambda.

**How to apply:** Find InboxPatchRequest (or similar) in services/inbox and add proper json tags. Rebuild and deploy inboxes Lambda.
close_reason: Already fixed in previous session — added json tags to UpdateInboxRequest in services/inbox/service/inbox_service.go

title: Integration test suite: comprehensive E2E tests for all nGX subsystems
description:
## Why this exists
The AWS serverless migration is functionally verified via manual smoke testing, but there are no automated integration tests. Any future code or infra change could silently break a subsystem. We need a repeatable test suite that runs against the deployed stack and catches regressions.

## What needs to be done
Create a Go test package at tests/integration/ that exercises every major subsystem end-to-end against the real deployed AWS stack (API GW endpoint from terraform output / env vars).

### Test cases to implement

**1. REST API — full CRUD coverage**
- Org: GET, PATCH
- Pods: create, get, update, delete
- API keys: create, get, delete
- Labels: create, update, delete
- Inboxes: create, get, update, delete
- Threads: list, get, patch (mark read/starred, change status)
- Messages: get single, get thread messages
- Drafts: create, update, approve (triggers send), reject, delete
- Webhooks: create, get, update, delete, list deliveries
- Search: keyword query returns expected results
- Domains: list, verify

**2. Outbound email (SES send)**
- POST /v1/inboxes/{id}/messages/send
- Verify message record status transitions: sending → sent (or bounced for fake address)
- Verify ses_events Lambda updates message status on SES delivery/bounce notification

**3. Inbound email (SES receive)**
- Upload a raw RFC 5322 .eml file to the S3 emails bucket under the SES receipt prefix
- Publish an S3 notification event to trigger email_inbound Lambda (or invoke directly)
- Verify thread + message records created in DB via API
- Verify MessageReceived and ThreadCreated events published (check WS or SQS)

**4. WebSocket event delivery**
- Connect to wss://... with valid ?token= API key
- Trigger a send message → verify message.bounced or message.sent event received on WS within 30s
- Verify stale connections are pruned on disconnect

**5. Webhook event delivery**
- Register webhook pointing to a real receiver (webhook.site or local httptest server)
- Send a message → verify event POST received at receiver within 30s
- Verify delivery record created with status=success and response_status=200
- Verify HMAC-SHA256 X-nGX-Signature header is present and valid

**6. Embedder + semantic search**
- Send a message with known body text
- Wait for embedder Lambda to process (SQS async)
- Run /v1/search?q=<text from body>
- Verify the message appears in results with non-zero rank

**7. Scheduler drafts**
- Create a draft with scheduled_at in the past (or invoke scheduler_drafts Lambda directly)
- Verify draft transitions to sent status

## Acceptance criteria
- All tests runnable with: TEST_API_KEY=... TEST_BASE_URL=... go test ./tests/integration/... -v -timeout 120s
- Tests are independent (each creates and cleans up its own resources)
- Tests skip gracefully if required env vars are missing
- Pass rate: 100% on the deployed prod stack at time of writing
close_reason: Integration test suite implemented and pushed: REST CRUD, outbound/inbound email, WebSocket, webhook delivery, search, and scheduler tests

title: SES DNS records: add TXT/MX/DKIM CNAMEs for mail.agent-mx.cc
description: SES domain identity verification requires: 1) TXT record for domain verification token. 2) MX record pointing to SES inbound endpoint. 3) 3x DKIM CNAME records for DKIM signing. Get values from terraform output ses_dkim_tokens and ses_verification_token. Add to DNS provider.

title: Integration test suite: end-to-end API tests against deployed AWS stack
description: Create tests/integration/ Go test package. Cover: 1) Create org + pod + API key (bootstrap). 2) Create inbox. 3) Send email (outbound). 4) Receive email via SES (inbound). 5) WebSocket connection + event delivery. 6) Search. 7) Webhook delivery. 8) Custom domain. All tests run against real deployed infra (API GW endpoint from terraform output).

title: Event Dispatcher WS Lambda: SQS trigger, PostToConnection via API GW Management API
description: Triggered by ws_dispatch SQS queue (batch 10). For each event: look up active websocket_connections for org. Call ApiGatewayManagementApi.PostToConnection for each live connection. Delete stale connections (GoneException). Env: APIGW_WEBSOCKET_ENDPOINT.

title: Email Outbound Lambda: SQS trigger, send via SES SendRawEmail
description: Triggered by email_outbound FIFO SQS queue. Read outbound job (message_id, from, to, subject, body, attachments). Compose RFC5322 MIME message. Call SES SendRawEmail with SES_CONFIGURATION_SET. Update message/outbound_job status. Publish delivery events to webhook+ws queues.

title: Email Inbound Lambda: S3 trigger, parse RFC5322, store to DB, fan events
description: Triggered by SQS email_inbound queue (populated by S3 ObjectCreated from SES receipt rules). Read raw email from S3 (S3_BUCKET_EMAILS). Parse MIME (reuse pkg/mime). Resolve inbox by recipient address. Create thread+message in DB. Publish events to webhook_delivery and ws_dispatch SQS queues.

title: WebSocket Lambdas: ws_connect and ws_disconnect
description: ws_connect: validate API key from query param, insert into websocket_connections table (connection_id, org_id, pod_id). ws_disconnect: delete from websocket_connections by connection_id. Both outside VPC for low latency. Need APIGW_WEBSOCKET_ENDPOINT env var.

title: REST API Lambdas: orgs, auth, inboxes, threads, messages, drafts, webhooks, search, domains
description: Implement 9 REST API Lambda functions using aws-lambda-go-api-proxy/chi bridge. Each reuses existing service/store packages. Replace kafka.Producer with SQS publisher for event fanout. Env vars injected by Terraform. Use provided.al2023 runtime with bootstrap binary.

title: Go Lambda: implement all 18 Lambda functions replacing Python stubs
description: Replace python3.12 placeholder zips with real Go provided.al2023 binaries. Lambdas: authorizer, orgs, auth, inboxes, threads, messages, drafts, webhooks, search, ws_connect, ws_disconnect, email_inbound, email_outbound, event_dispatcher_webhook, event_dispatcher_ws, embedder, ses_events, scheduler_drafts, domains. Need make build-lambdas and make deploy-lambdas Makefile targets.

title: event_dispatcher_ws: set APIGW_WEBSOCKET_ENDPOINT env var post-deploy
description: Run: aws lambda update-function-configuration --function-name ngx-prod-event-dispatcher-ws --environment Variables={APIGW_WEBSOCKET_ENDPOINT=wss://ynrnrj9006.execute-api.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/prod}. Needed before WebSocket dispatch works.

title: SES DNS: add TXT verification + MX + 3 DKIM CNAME records for mail.agent-mx.cc
description: Add DNS records at domain registrar for mail.agent-mx.cc. TXT: _amazonses.mail.agent-mx.cc = hFiYtr/yfkrLvMU3llHZee+h+4UcyUr4ybgar5IKszQ=. MX: mail.agent-mx.cc -> inbound-smtp.us-east-1.amazonaws.com. DKIM CNAMEs: tokens 7gmfkpbqx5ajgt3fbxvf7xznhiakxkdd, v7oxm5atzor67pvvwwdrpidesg4zvolt, zjue7bnl4ll27v6cvjwjwuixdcfthuhp. Until done, SES cannot receive inbound email.

title: Bootstrap: create org, pod, API key, seed domain for mail.agent-mx.cc
description: Ran tools/bootstrap against Aurora via SSM tunnel. org=nyklabs (ID: 4016a9af-aecc-4b45-b134-64c8ae557571), pod=default (bc3afa42-3631-4e95-ba39-a719301aee6c), domain_config mail.agent-mx.cc status=active. API key saved in Secrets Manager.
close_reason: Bootstrap complete. Org, pod, API key, and domain_config seeded.

title: Terraform apply: fix plan errors and deploy all 350 resources
description: Fixed 5 errors: SG non-ASCII descriptions, API GW CloudWatch role, method_settings resource type, S3 lifecycle filter, SES output vars. Applied to nyk-tf account. Imported stale state for Lambda permission and Secrets Manager secret after partial applies.
close_reason: All errors fixed, state reconciled, apply succeeded.

title: Database: run 17 migrations against Aurora via RDS Proxy
description: Set up SSM tunnel via bastion (t2.micro free tier). Connected psql using Homebrew OpenSSL CA bundle at /usr/local/etc/openssl@3/cert.pem on port 15432. Fixed migration 016 partial index using NOW() (not IMMUTABLE). All 16 tables created.
close_reason: All 17 migrations applied. 16 tables live on Aurora including websocket_connections.

title: Terraform: full AWS serverless infrastructure (VPC, Aurora, SQS, SES, API GW, Lambda stubs)
description: Replace self-hosted Kafka/Redis/SMTP stack with AWS serverless. Includes VPC, Aurora Serverless v2 + RDS Proxy, SQS queues, SES inbound/outbound, API Gateway REST + WebSocket, 18 Lambda stubs, EventBridge, IAM, S3, CloudWatch.
close_reason: Terraform infrastructure fully applied to nyk-tf account. 350 resources created.

title: Terraform: adopt SES for inbound+outbound, drop SNS fan-out, simplify EventBridge
description: Update Terraform foundation to: (1) use SES for both inbound and outbound email replacing custom MTA, (2) remove SNS events-fanout — app Lambdas publish directly to SQS queues, (3) remove EventBridge bounce-check rule since SES handles bounces via configuration set, (4) add SES configuration set + ses-events SNS/SQS for bounce/complaint delivery notifications, (5) set AWS provider profile=nyk-tf, (6) add websocket_connections Postgres migration.
close_reason: All Terraform changes committed and pushed: SES full MTA, SNS fan-out removed, EventBridge simplified to draft expiry only, ses_events Lambda added, migration 000016 for websocket_connections

title: Add setup-env.sh — full environment bootstrap script
description: Counterpart to destroy-env.sh. Stands up a complete nGX environment from scratch: builds Lambda ZIPs, runs terraform init+apply, syncs env, waits for bastion SSM readiness, runs DB migrations via SSM tunnel, deploys lambdas, bootstraps initial org, and prints post-deploy DNS records (SES DKIM + verification).

title: Spec+Test: add implemented Domains API paths to OpenAPI and verify coverage
description: Domains endpoints are implemented and integration-tested, but the OpenAPI path surface does not currently describe these routes. This creates blind spots in contract-driven coverage and client generation.
acceptance_criteria: 1) OpenAPI paths include implemented domains endpoints and request/response schemas. 2) Integration checks include at least one contract assertion per domains route. 3) API docs and generated clients are updated/validated against the revised spec.

title: Fix+Test: validate draft recipients/input fields to match API contract
description: Draft create/update paths are missing integration coverage for required recipient validity and minimum constraints. This can allow malformed draft payloads to pass or fail inconsistently versus documented API behavior.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Draft create enforces required recipient constraints and rejects invalid addresses with 400. 2) Draft update enforces same validity rules for updated recipient fields. 3) Integration tests cover missing/empty/invalid recipient cases and one valid case.

title: Test: draft list pagination contract (limit/cursor + cursor response metadata)
description: Draft listing currently has no integration assertions for pagination semantics or cursor response metadata. Tests only verify non-empty list behavior, which can miss regressions in cursoring and envelope fields.
acceptance_criteria: 1) Integration tests cover first/next page behavior for /v1/inboxes/{inboxId}/drafts with limit and cursor. 2) Tests assert response metadata contract for pagination (cursor field naming and behavior). 3) Invalid/terminal cursor behavior is covered with deterministic assertions.

title: Test Infra: add OpenAPI contract checks to integration suite to catch spec/runtime drift
description: The integration suite currently validates behavior but does not enforce API contract consistency against api/openapi.yaml. Multiple field and schema drifts are possible without failing CI (request field names, response envelope fields, and documented parameters).
acceptance_criteria: 1) Add integration contract coverage that validates key request/response shapes and documented fields against OpenAPI for critical endpoints. 2) Fail CI when runtime contract diverges from OpenAPI (or explicitly allowlisted exceptions). 3) Document workflow for updating contract tests when API intentionally changes.

title: Test: search result snippet field never asserted
description:
Integration tests assert subject, thread_id, inbox_id, rank on search results but never verify the snippet field is populated. The snippet should contain a short excerpt of matching text.

What to test:
- Send message with distinctive body text
- Search for a term that appears in the body
- Assert snippet field is non-empty
- Assert snippet contains the search term or surrounding context

title: Test: PATCH /org with empty display_name returns 400
description:
The patchOrg handler returns 400 if name is empty, but no test exercises this validation path. Also verify that the JSON field name expected by the handler (name vs display_name) matches what the OpenAPI spec documents.

What to test:
- PATCH /org with {display_name: ''} → assert 400
- PATCH /org with {} (no fields) → assert 400 or 200 with no change (document behavior)
- Verify field name alignment between OpenAPI spec and handler JSON tag

title: Test: webhook delivery cursor pagination not tested
description:
GET /webhooks/{id}/deliveries supports cursor-based pagination per the OpenAPI spec, but the pagination path has no integration test.

What to test:
- Register webhook pointing to httpbin
- Trigger 3+ events to generate multiple delivery records
- GET /webhooks/{id}/deliveries?limit=2 → assert 2 records returned and cursor present
- Follow cursor → assert remaining records returned
- Assert no duplicates across pages

title: Test: BCC field — recipients receive email, BCC not exposed in headers
description:
A comment in the message service explains BCC is read from the DB by the outbound pipeline, not passed in the queue payload. No test verifies BCC behavior end-to-end.

What to test:
- Send message with BCC recipient
- GET message → assert bcc field is present in API response
- Verify the outbound email RFC headers do not expose BCC recipients (BCC must be stripped from message headers before delivery)
- Verify the BCC recipient address appears in the SES send call (via sent status)

title: Test: inbound email with HTML body (multipart/alternative) not tested
description:
TestInboundEmail only exercises plain-text emails. No test verifies a multipart/alternative inbound email (text + HTML parts) is stored correctly with both body fields populated.

What to test:
- Build a multipart/alternative EML with both text/plain and text/html parts
- Inject via the inbound email pipeline (S3 + Lambda invoke)
- GET message → assert both body_text and body_html are populated
- Verify preview is derived from the text part

title: Fix+Test: GET /inboxes ignores ?limit= query param (hardcoded 50)
description:
lambdas/inboxes/main.go hardcodes limit: 50 regardless of the ?limit= query parameter. api/openapi.yaml documents limit as a valid parameter. No test passes ?limit= and verifies it is honored.

What to fix:
- Read ?limit= from query params in the inboxes lambda and pass it to the store

What to test:
- Create 3+ inboxes
- GET /inboxes?limit=2 → assert 2 items returned and next_page_token present
- Follow cursor → assert remaining inboxes returned

title: Fix+Test: POST /pods slug pattern not validated server-side
description:
api/openapi.yaml requires slug pattern ^[a-z0-9-]+$. lambdas/pods inserts the slug directly without validating the pattern. No test sends an invalid slug.

What to fix:
- Add slug pattern validation (reject uppercase, spaces, special chars) before DB insert

What to test:
- POST /pods with slug 'My Pod\!' → assert 400
- POST /pods with slug 'UPPERCASE' → assert 400
- POST /pods with slug 'valid-slug-123' → assert 201

title: Test: duplicate label name returns 409 not 500
description:
api/openapi.yaml documents 409 for duplicate label names. TestDuplicateConflicts covers inbox address and pod slug duplicates but not label name.

What to test:
- Create label with name 'urgent'
- Create another label with name 'urgent'
- Assert 409 Conflict (not 500)
- Assert first label is unaffected

title: Test: API key with expires_at in the past is rejected (401)
description:
The authorizer SQL enforces expires_at > NOW() but no integration test creates an expired key and verifies authentication is rejected.

What to test:
- Create API key with expires_at set to a timestamp 1-2 seconds in the future
- Wait for expiry
- Attempt any authenticated request with the expired key
- Assert 401 Unauthorized

title: Test: thread PATCH with multiple fields simultaneously
description:
The thread PATCH handler processes status, is_read, and is_starred in separate if-branches. Only the first matching field in the handler logic is guaranteed to be applied. Sending {status:closed, is_starred:true} may only update status.

What to test:
- PATCH thread with {is_read:true, is_starred:true} simultaneously
- GET thread and assert BOTH fields are updated
- If only one field is applied per call, document this limitation with a test comment and add a note to the OpenAPI spec

title: Fix: add dispatcher-side assertions to engagement event integration tests
description: The Email Opened and Email Clicked integration tests in tests/integration/ses_events_test.go only verify that message status does not change. They never exercise the webhook/websocket dispatcher path, so the missing Unmarshal case for message.engagement (now fixed in pkg/events/marshal.go) went undetected. The tests need webhook delivery record assertions to confirm engagement events flow all the way through the dispatcher pipeline.
close_reason: Added wantEventType field to test cases; engagement cases now register a webhook before injecting the SES event and poll delivery records to verify the full dispatcher pipeline runs. Delivery Delayed keeps the sleep-based no-status-change check since no event is published.

title: ses_events Lambda: publish domain events on status transitions
description:
## Context

The ses_events Lambda currently only updates the messages table in the DB. It does not publish domain events (MessageBouncedEvent, MessageFailedEvent) to the webhook or WebSocket queues. This means:
- Webhook subscribers never receive real-time bounce/delivery notifications
- WebSocket clients cannot update UI state when delivery status changes
- Observability / audit trail gaps for bounce/complaint events

## What needs to be done

1. Wire event publisher in ses_events Lambda (WEBHOOK_DELIVERY_QUEUE_URL and WS_DISPATCH_QUEUE_URL already in Lambda env vars per lambda.tf)
2. After DB update, publish:
   - Email Bounced → MessageBouncedEvent (already defined in pkg/events/email_events.go)
   - Email Complaint Received → a new MessageFailedEvent or reuse MessageBouncedEvent with type=complaint
   - Email Delivered → note: email_outbound already publishes MessageSentEvent on send — confirm if double-publish is an issue or gate on prior status
3. Add EventMessageFailed event type to pkg/events/types.go if needed
4. Update email_events.go with any new event structs
5. Add unit tests for event publishing

## Dependencies
Blocked by nGX-jjk (correct event strings must be in place first)

## Acceptance criteria
- Bounce/complaint/delivery transitions publish domain events
- Webhooks and WS dispatch receive these events
- No duplicate events for the delivery case
close_reason: Publisher wired in ses_events Lambda; RETURNING used to get msg/org UUIDs; loadMsg helper loads full message+attachments+S3 bodies; publishMsgBounced/publishMsgSent/publishMsgEngagement helpers publish fat domain events for all 6 event types.

title: Expand SES event coverage: handle all 8 EventBridge event types
description:
## Context

AWS SES publishes 8 event types via EventBridge. We currently handle 3. The remaining 5 are silently ignored.

## Full event type mapping

| SES EventBridge event      | Internal action                         | Status change     |
|----------------------------|-----------------------------------------|-------------------|
| Email Bounced              | ✅ Done (nGX-jjk)                       | → bounced         |
| Email Complaint Received   | ✅ Done (nGX-jjk)                       | → failed          |
| Email Delivered            | ✅ Done (nGX-jjk)                       | → sent            |
| Email Rejected             | ✅ Done — Lambda + EventBridge filter   | → failed          |
| Email Rendering Failed     | ✅ Done — Lambda + EventBridge filter   | → failed          |
| Email Delivery Delayed     | ✅ Done — log-only, no status change    | (none)            |
| Email Clicked              | ⬜ Remaining — needs engagement tracking enabled in ses.tf config set first |
| Email Opened               | ⬜ Remaining — same as Clicked          |

## Remaining work

1. Enable engagement tracking in aws_ses_configuration_set in ses.tf
2. Add 'Email Clicked' and 'Email Opened' to EventBridge detail-type filter
3. Define EventMessageEngagement in pkg/events/types.go
4. Add engagement event struct to email_events.go
5. Publish engagement event from ses_events Lambda for these two types
6. Unit tests for the new cases

## Notes
- Click/open tracking requires SES configuration set to have engagement tracking enabled in ses.tf
- Without engagement tracking, SES never fires these events even if they are in the filter
close_reason: Email Clicked and Email Opened fully implemented: tracking_options{} added to ses.tf, both types added to EventBridge filter, ebClick/ebOpen sub-structs parsed, MessageEngagementEvent added to pkg/events, engagement events published with link_url/ip_address/user_agent.

title: Wire TEST_LAMBDA_PREFIX and TEST_AWS_REGION into integration test environment
description:
Three integration tests are permanently skipped because TEST_LAMBDA_PREFIX and TEST_AWS_REGION are not set: TestSchedulerDrafts, TestSESEventsBounce, TestSESEventsDelivery, TestInboundSemanticSearch. These cover critical async workflows.

What to do:
- Determine correct values: TEST_LAMBDA_PREFIX=ngx-prod, TEST_AWS_REGION=us-east-1
- Add to the test runner script or Makefile target
- Verify all four previously-skipped tests now run and pass
- Document the full set of env vars needed to run the complete integration suite

title: Integration test: SES bounce and delivery events end-to-end
description:
TestSESEventsBounce and TestSESEventsDelivery are always skipped because TEST_LAMBDA_PREFIX and TEST_AWS_REGION are not set. These cover critical message status transitions (sent→failed on bounce, sent→sent on delivery confirmation).

NOTE: The integration tests also inject synthetic events using wrong detail-type strings ('SES Bounce', 'SES Message Delivery') — this must be fixed as part of nGX-jjk before these tests can pass even when env vars are set.

What to test:
- Publish synthetic EventBridge 'Email Bounced' event to ses_events queue
- Verify message status transitions to 'bounced' (after nGX-gfn) or 'failed' (interim)
- Publish synthetic EventBridge 'Email Delivered' event
- Verify message status transitions to 'sent'

Requires:
- nGX-jjk: fix event type strings in test payloads
- TEST_LAMBDA_PREFIX and TEST_AWS_REGION wired into test run (same pattern as scheduler test)

title: Integration test: webhook HMAC-SHA256 signature validation
description:
TestWebhookEventDelivery receives the webhook payload locally but never verifies the HMAC-SHA256 signature. If the signing key rotates or the signature logic breaks, no test would catch it.

What to test:
- Receive webhook POST → verify X-Webhook-Signature header is present
- Compute expected HMAC-SHA256(secret, body) → verify it matches
- Verify a delivery attempt with wrong signature would be rejected (if the server validates inbound)
- Test that signature changes when webhook secret is rotated

title: Integration test: scheduler drafts Lambda end-to-end
description:
TestSchedulerDrafts is always skipped because TEST_LAMBDA_PREFIX and TEST_AWS_REGION env vars are not wired in. The scheduled draft → auto-approve → sent flow has never been verified end-to-end.

What to test:
- Create draft with scheduled_at = now - 1 minute
- Invoke scheduler_drafts Lambda directly
- Verify draft status transitions to approved
- Verify message is created and queued for sending

Requires: TEST_LAMBDA_PREFIX and TEST_AWS_REGION to be set in test environment.
Action needed: wire these env vars into the test run.

title: Integration test: attachment edge cases
description:
Attachment happy path (upload + send + receive) is tested, but failure modes are not.

What to test:
- Oversized attachment (e.g. 20MB base64 payload) → expect 400 or graceful rejection
- Invalid base64 content → expect 400
- Attachment with empty filename → verify behavior
- Verify S3 key format is correct (orgID/msgID/filename) by checking attachment URL or metadata
- Verify draft attachment cleanup on draft delete (cascade)

title: Integration test: outbound email input validation
description:
The send message Lambda should reject invalid inputs with 400, but no tests verify this. Broken validation would either crash the Lambda or send malformed emails to SES.

What to test:
- Send with empty to array → expect 400
- Send with invalid email address in to → expect 400
- Send with empty body_text and body_html → expect 400
- Send with missing subject → verify behavior (400 or allowed)
- Send with oversized attachment (>10MB) → expect 400

title: Integration test: duplicate and conflict error handling
description:
Unique constraints in the DB are untested. If constraint enforcement is broken (e.g. missing index, wrong column) the API would return a 500 instead of a proper 400/409.

What to test:
- Register same domain twice → expect 400/409
- Create two inboxes with same address → expect 400/409
- Create two pods with same slug → expect 400/409
- Verify error message is user-friendly, not a raw DB error

title: Integration test: inbound email with attachments
description: TestInboundEmail only sends a plain-text EML. Need a test that builds a multipart/mixed EML with a file attachment, invokes email_inbound Lambda directly, and verifies the message is stored and attachments are accessible via GET /v1/inboxes/{id}/threads/{tid}/messages/{mid}/attachments.
close_reason: TestInboundEmailWithAttachment passes; fixed email_store.go using msg.HasAttachments instead of len(msg.Attachments)

title: Integration test: draft rejection flow
description: TestDrafts covers draft approve but not reject. Need a test that creates a draft, rejects it via POST /v1/drafts/{id}/reject, and verifies the draft status transitions to rejected and a DraftRejectedEvent is delivered via webhook.
close_reason: TestDraftRejection added and passing (4 subtests: status_is_rejected, get_returns_rejected_draft, rejected_draft_not_in_pending_list, cannot_approve_rejected_draft)

title: Fix search pagination cursor for outbound messages (NULL received_at)
description: Bug found during integration testing. Keyword search pagination returns empty second page. Root cause: outbound messages have NULL received_at (only inbound messages have it). The cursor is (received_at, id) but NULL received_at makes the cursor comparison fail. Fix: use COALESCE(received_at, sent_at, created_at) in the cursor field for keywordSearch in lambdas/search/main.go. TestSearchPagination fails.
close_reason: Fixed and verified by integration tests passing

title: Fix GET /v1/domains/:id returns 404
description: Bug found during integration testing. GET /v1/domains/:id returns 404 even when domain exists in list. Likely the route handler for GET by ID is missing in lambdas/domains/main.go. TestDomains/get: expected HTTP 200, got 404.
close_reason: Fixed and verified by integration tests passing

title: Scheduler Drafts Lambda: EventBridge 5-min trigger, expire pending drafts
description: Triggered by EventBridge rule every 5 minutes. Find drafts where scheduled_at < now() and status=pending. For each: publish to email_outbound FIFO queue. Update draft status to queued. Reuse services/scheduler draft expiry logic adapted for Lambda.

title: SES Events Lambda: bounce/complaint/delivery processing
description: Triggered by ses_events SQS queue. Process SES notification types: Bounce (mark message failed, update outbound_job status), Complaint (record complaint), Delivery (mark message delivered). Publish status change events to webhook+ws queues.

title: Embedder Lambda: SQS trigger, generate vector embeddings, store to DB
description: Triggered by embedder SQS queue (batch 1). Read message content from DB. Call EMBEDDER_URL (HTTP) to generate vector. Store embedding in messages.embedding column (pgvector). Implements 90-day semantic search: only embed recent messages, run cleanup job to remove embeddings older than 90 days.

title: Event Dispatcher Webhook Lambda: SQS trigger, HTTP POST to webhook endpoints
description: Triggered by webhook_delivery SQS queue (batch 10). For each event: look up webhook subscriptions for org. HTTP POST event payload with HMAC-SHA256 signature. Retry logic (exponential backoff). Record delivery attempt. Reuse pkg/crypto for signature. Env: WEBHOOK_ENCRYPTION_KEY.

title: Custom domain API: POST|GET|DELETE /v1/domains + /verify for enterprise BYOD
description: Enterprise customers register acme.com so agents get myagent@acme.com. Flow: POST /v1/domains -> SES VerifyDomainIdentity + VerifyDomainDkim + CreateReceiptRule + DB insert -> returns 5 DNS records (TXT+MX+3xCNAME). POST /v1/domains/{id}/verify polls SES status. Services: services/domains/ (handler/service/store). Lambda: ngx-prod-domains. IAM: SESDomainManagement perms added.
close_reason: Code and infra deployed. Lambda stub in place. Go implementation tracked under nGX-275.

title: Bastion: t2.micro EC2 with SSM Session Manager for private subnet access
description: Free-tier t2.micro Amazon Linux 2 in public subnet. SSM-only access (no SSH key, no open ports). IAM role AmazonSSMManagedInstanceCore. Bastion SG added to RDS Proxy ingress. Instance: i-0a690d4de643a6627. SSM tunnel cmd: aws ssm start-session --target i-0a690d4de643a6627 --document-name AWS-StartPortForwardingSessionToRemoteHost --parameters host=ngx-prod-proxy...port=5432,localPort=15432
close_reason: Bastion deployed. SSM tunnel confirmed working. psql connects via 127.0.0.1:15432.

title: Epic: refactoring nGX to use AWS servless infra
close_reason: Empty parent epic for the AWS serverless migration — all child work completed on aws-serverless-stack branch

title: Integration test: search edge cases (empty query, special characters, no results)
description:
Keyword and semantic search happy paths are tested, but edge cases that could cause 500 errors or incorrect results are not.

What to test:
- Empty query string → expect 400 or empty results (not 500)
- Query with SQL special characters (%,',") → verify no injection, returns empty results
- Query with no matching results → verify empty array returned, not null
- Very long query string (>1000 chars) → verify graceful handling
- Search before any messages exist → verify empty results

title: Integration test: inbox status transitions (active/suspended)
description:
Inbox create/read/update/delete is tested but status field changes are not. Suspending an inbox should stop email routing; no test verifies this.

What to test:
- PATCH inbox status to suspended → verify GET returns status=suspended
- Verify inbound email to suspended inbox is rejected or dropped
- PATCH back to active → verify email routing resumes
- DELETE inbox → verify all associated threads/messages are not accessible

title: Integration test: custom domain SES receipt rule provisioning
description: POST /v1/domains provisions a custom email domain: creates SES receipt rule, DKIM records, and domain config in DB. No integration test covers this flow end-to-end because it requires DNS delegation of the domain to SES (MX + TXT records). Hard to test in automated CI without a dedicated test domain with pre-delegated DNS. File for tracking — could be tested manually or with a dedicated throwaway domain in the test AWS account.
close_reason: TestCustomDomainProvisioning added and passing — register, DNS records (1 TXT + 1 MX + 3 CNAME), status=pending, list, get, delete lifecycle all verified against live AWS stack using mail.nyklabs.com as throwaway domain

title: Integration test: webhook retry and DLQ on delivery failure
description: No test covers the webhook retry path. When a webhook endpoint returns 5xx the delivery should be retried with backoff up to WEBHOOK_MAX_RETRIES times, then land in DLQ. Hard to test in integration suite since it requires a publicly reachable HTTP server returning controlled failure responses from inside AWS Lambda VPC. Options: (1) deploy a dedicated test Lambda that returns 500, (2) unit test the retry logic directly. File for tracking — implement when a mock endpoint strategy is decided.
notes: Deferred — webhook DLQ endpoint not wired in integration test environment

title: Generate .env.outputs from terraform outputs post-deploy
description:
Post-deploy values (SQS URLs, API endpoints, DATABASE_URL) are hand-copied into .env from terraform apply output. This creates silent drift when infra is redeployed.

What needs doing:
1. Add missing terraform outputs: sqs_ws_dispatch_url, sqs_embedder_url
2. Write scripts/sync-env.sh — runs terraform output -json + aws secretsmanager get-secret-value to generate .env.outputs with all post-deploy values
3. Update loadenv.sh to source .env.outputs if present
4. Update .env.example to clearly separate pre-deploy (TF_VAR_* + static config) from post-deploy vars (populated by sync-env.sh), with a comment pointing at the script
5. Clean up .env: remove standalone WEBHOOK_ENCRYPTION_KEY (duplicate of TF_VAR_webhook_encryption_key; Lambdas get it from terraform)

Acceptance: after terraform apply, running scripts/sync-env.sh && source loadenv.sh gives a fully populated env with no manual copy-paste. .env only contains values a human needs to supply before first deploy.
close_reason: Implemented and tested against live stack

title: Documentation: update README and AGENTS.md for AWS serverless architecture
description: Update README.md: replace Docker compose/Kafka/Redis setup with AWS serverless deployment guide. Add: terraform apply steps, Lambda deployment, SES DNS setup, API GW endpoints. Update AGENTS.md with new architecture. Remove references to SMTP, Kafka, Redis that no longer apply.
close_reason: README.md and AGENTS.md updated for AWS serverless architecture. Two-file .env model documented. .env.example already has the workflow steps.

title: TF_VAR_* in .env: document and test per-enterprise config workflow
description: Operators copy .env.example to .env, fill TF_VAR_* vars, run 'source loadenv.sh && AWS_PROFILE=<profile> terraform plan'. No -var flags or terraform.tfvars needed. Profile nyk-tf defaults to us-west-2 — always pass --region us-east-1 to AWS CLI commands.
close_reason: README.md and AGENTS.md updated for AWS serverless architecture. Two-file .env model documented. .env.example already has the workflow steps.

title: Test: draft scheduled_at round-trips correctly through GET
description:
TestSchedulerDrafts creates a draft with scheduled_at and invokes the scheduler lambda, but never does a GET to verify scheduled_at is returned correctly in the draft response before processing.

What to test:
- Create draft with scheduled_at set 5 minutes in the future
- GET /drafts/{id} → assert scheduled_at matches the value set
- Assert scheduled_at field is in RFC3339 format
- Assert the draft status is still 'pending' before the scheduler runs

title: Test: GET /keys list pagination with enough keys to trigger next_page_token
description:
GET /keys returns an APIKeyList with next_page_token per the spec, but no test creates enough keys to exercise pagination. The lambda may not implement pagination at all (returns a plain list).

What to test:
- Create enough API keys to exceed the default page size
- GET /keys → assert next_page_token is present
- Follow next_page_token → assert remaining keys returned
- Assert no duplicates across pages

title: Fix+Test: label color field format not validated server-side
description:
api/openapi.yaml describes the color field as a hex color code, but the label service performs no format validation. Any string value is accepted and stored.

What to fix:
- Add hex color validation (e.g. ^#[0-9a-fA-F]{6}$) to the label create/update handlers

What to test:
- POST /labels with color 'not-a-hex' → assert 400
- POST /labels with color '#gg0000' → assert 400
- POST /labels with color '#ff5733' → assert 201

title: Test: thread PATCH with empty body — undefined behavior
description:
Sending PATCH /threads/{id} with {} (no recognized fields) falls through the handler with no update and returns an ambiguous response. The correct behavior (200 with no change, or 400) is undocumented and untested.

What to test:
- PATCH thread with {} → document and assert the actual behavior
- PATCH thread with {unknown_field: value} → assert 400 (unrecognized field) or 200 (ignored)
- Update OpenAPI spec to clarify which fields are required vs optional

title: Fix+Test: pod PATCH settings field silently ignored
description:
The pod PATCH handler only updates name and description. The settings field in UpdatePodRequest is present in the model but ignored by the handler — updates to settings are silently dropped.

What to fix:
- Handle settings field in the pod PATCH handler

What to test:
- Create pod with settings
- PATCH pod with new settings
- GET pod → assert settings field reflects the update
